title: sudo podman create: error creating container storage: error creating ID-mapped copy of layer "8042eb3a896959439cf57f9e6c964552292c90a8540048fa2efb80cef5d5feec": exit status 1: error during chown: storage-chown-by-maps: chown("usr/bin/ping"): no data available
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]:  **kind bug**


**Description**

Running podman create with sudo fails to create a container.
Running the command without sudo (rootless) creates the container, but  I run into another issue (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1240).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sh -x /usr/bin/fedora-toolbox --sudo -v create`


**Describe the results you received:**

**Describe the results you expected:**
Succesful creation of container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I tried wiping .local/share/containers and /var/lib/containers, but the problem is still there.
I fodun this while debugging https://pagure.io/teamsilverblue/issue/61

comments.body:
I am also running into this. I have reported this directly against the fedora-toolbox repo but posting here was probably what I should have done.

https://github.com/debarshiray/fedora-toolbox/issues/25

@seandjones92 Fedora toolbox is usually run rootless - are you hitting this issue when running rootless, or when run as root?

@giuseppe Given that it's the `ping` binary I'm imagining it's the extra handling for file capabilities?

@mheon I have attempted to run rootless multiple times, only ran once with root as a test and it still failed. I can reproduce this on demand either on my machine or in a 'vanilla' VM. I'd be more than happy to assist with gathering further information or testing. I'm running Fedora Silverblue **EDIT:** When I run `fedora-toolbox` with and without the `--sudo` flag I get two different results. Without `--sudo` ``` ➜ fedora-toolbox git:(master) ./fedora-toolbox -v create error reading image "fedora-toolbox-sean:29": error reading image: error locating image "fedora-toolbox-sean:29" for importing settings: error locating image with name "fedora-toolbox-sean:29": image not known ERRO[0000] exit status 1 Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://localhost/fedora-toolbox:29: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://localhost/v2/: dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused ERRO[0000] exit status 1 Getting image source signatures Copying blob sha256:af19ce19de5ee70d1ca852c65f9927fab6ba09de2864af2acdf18d60774bffbd 85.70 MiB / 85.70 MiB [=================================================] 3m10s Copying blob sha256:6df1bfffa76ae08ebcb61de2ae28fbf5f8a84079b6cb316d095cdf4cb5e2bdbb 182.56 MiB / 182.56 MiB [=================================================] 19s Copying config sha256:032b427fbbf72ac22336638af319ef989a2b99e038ca087743c132ab71445ed8 2.69 KiB / 2.69 KiB [======================================================] 0s Writing manifest to image destination Storing signatures passwd: Note: deleting a password also unlocks the password. passwd: Note: deleting a password also unlocks the password. Getting image source signatures Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:8080f9aa6262000ad12d3d7e55331d275d412faa730b75c41bbf444b4ce056e9 Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:d399ea65472cbad41d640ec2a09724c2f11ac7fa52636b6cec6905e8fa490865 Copying blob sha256:815b37bd26c8dbc39d984a83f2cf31e2932c12ea7adbc13e00b321eee249273e 1.96 KiB / 1.96 KiB [======================================================] 0s Copying config sha256:a4725eca852efd3b9dce923c19f8f25cae254dbb5fbecdb03b7e18c0c8dff42c 1.43 KiB / 1.43 KiB [======================================================] 0s Writing manifest to image destination Storing signatures error looking up container "fedora-toolbox-sean:29": no container with name or ID fedora-toolbox-sean:29 found: no such container error creating container storage: error creating ID-mapped copy of layer "8042eb3a896959439cf57f9e6c964552292c90a8540048fa2efb80cef5d5feec": exit status 1: error during chown: storage-chown-by-maps: chown("usr/bin/ping"): no data available ./fedora-toolbox: failed to create container fedora-toolbox-sean:29 ```

it looks like an overlayfs bug.  Probably it is related to the copyup for metadata only, as I can see it happening only with files having file capabilities and size > 0:

Simple reproducer:

```bash
ROOT=/tmp/test-root
rm -rf $ROOT
mkdir -p $ROOT/{u,l1,w,merged}
echo hello > $ROOT/l1/foo
setcap cap_setuid+ep $ROOT/l1/foo
mount -t overlay overlay -o rw,lowerdir=$ROOT/l1,upperdir=$ROOT/u,workdir=$ROOT/w $ROOT/merged
chown 1:1 $ROOT/merged/foo
umount $ROOT/merged
```

and the output:

```
$ sudo ./mount.sh 
chown: changing ownership of '/tmp/test-root/merged/foo': No data available
```

/cc @rhvgoyal

@giuseppe Ok, I think I have figured out what's the problem. It happens without enabling metacopy. But metacopy patches have introduced this issue. In one of the patches, I had changed order in which data and metadata is copied up. I started copying up metadata first and then data.

So if a file has setuid cap, security.capability is copied up. But as soon as data is copied up, that security.capability is cleared. And that means if you do "touch foo.txt" above, after copy up we lose
the setuid bit (which we should not have).

I guess I need to change the order back to copying up data first and then xattrs. Will have a temporary fix but need to run bunch of tests to make sure I am not breaking anything else.
Ok will have to get this fix into RHEL8 as well as Fedora.
After talking to @rhvgoyal this is still being discussed in the upstream kernel.  Will need to get fixes approved and into distributed kernel before this can be marked fixed.

@rhatdan Kernel fix is queued in overlayfs-next branch of miklos's tree. Waiting for him to send it to Linus.

https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/mszeredi/vfs.git/commit/?h=overlayfs-next&id=5f32879ea35523b9842bdbdc0065e13635caada2
i believe this is fixed, reopen if needed
